jobs.title: Principal Air Quality Consultant
jobs.description:
Job Description

What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?

At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.

WSP’s Atmospheric Service’s team is nationally renowned for its expertise in providing air quality, greenhouse gas and odour consultancy services to our broad client base. We have a current need for an experienced Air Quality Consultant to join our Victorian team based out of our central Melbourne office.

As An Air Quality Consultant You’ll

Undertake technical studies including:
Preparation of air quality, GHG and odour emission inventories
Analysis of technical data associated with air quality, GHG and odour assessments
Meteorological analysis and data preparation for air quality and odour dispersion modelling

Preparation of technical reports and air quality and greenhouse gas components of environmental impact assessments
Undertake laboratory and field work including:
Becoming proficient in WSP’s NATA-accredited air quality and odour sampling and analysis techniques
Emissions measurement and source testing
Air quality and meteorological monitoring
Laboratory analysis of air quality and odour field samples

Preparation of NATA-accredited sampling and analysis reports
Assist in the preparation of expert advice presented during stakeholder and regulator engagement activities related to air quality, GHGs, and odour.
Prepare technical proposals and cost estimates to complete air quality and related projects of low to moderate complexity.
Assist with the preparation of proposals and cost estimates for large projects and/or projects of moderate to high complexity
Liaise with clients, subcontractors, regulators, and other stakeholders while serving as Project Manager for air quality and related projects.
Assist teams in other offices in Australia, New Zealand and Southeast Asia to complete air quality and related projects
Assist with business development activities regionally within Australia
Keep up-to-date with developments in your professional area, for example through our internal technical communities or externally through trade organizations such as the Clean Air Society of Australia and New Zealand (CASANZ).

What You’ll Bring

Bachelor’s degree in chemical, civil, mechanical or environmental engineering, or a bachelor’s degree in physics, chemistry, or an environmental science.
3 to 5 years’ experience in air quality consulting or a graduate degree in meteorology or air quality.
Current, clean driver licence (full or restricted preferred).
Electro-mechanically inclined; e.g., instrument and/or lab-based experience.
Field-based monitoring and sampling experience in; VOCs , Dust , Particulate Matter and O dour
Proficient in MS Outlook/Word/Excel ideally with familiarity with Python, R or MATLAB
Willingness to travel as required and work collaboratively in a diverse problem solving focused team

Why WSP?

Imagine a better future for you, and for us all.

Joining WSP means becoming part of a global team of over 73,000,united by a shared passion for making a positive impact.

Join one of Australia's strongest Atmospheric Services teams supporting clients and projects across a variety of sectors.
Deliver work that genuinely shapes communities, grounded in WSP’s mission to provide sustainable solutions with deep environmental and social responsibility.
Grow in a culture built on collaboration, inclusion, and belonging, where diverse perspectives power innovation and employees feel valued and supported.
Enjoy work/life flexibility and strong career pathways, plus global mobility, health and wellbeing programs, and social initiatives.

Discover more on our https://www.wsp.com/en-au/careers/why-join-us/employee-benefits page.

With us, you can. Apply today.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Quality Assurance

Industries

Professional Services
